spec_match.rationale: The page now drives Chrome's real HTML byte-stream decoder with a blob iframe, reads `document.characterSet`/`document.inputEncoding`, and compares it with explicit `TextDecoder("iso-2022-jp")` output.

interactive.rationale: The workbench has sample-byte, HTTP charset, meta charset, editable hex, scenario buttons, a live iframe preview, status cards, and a before/after parser comparison table.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Concept page lede restates and delivers on the framing used in the feature index card.

edge_cases.rationale: Controls cover undeclared ISO-2022-JP bytes, explicit ISO-2022-JP, UTF-8 bytes, HTTP/meta conflicts, escaped script-shaped text, and invalid hex input.

references.rationale: Page cites the required chromestatus.com/feature/6576566521561088 self-heal link (CLAUDE.md rule 3).

design_system.rationale: Uses CSS variables throughout, no raw hex of note, and button overrides keep background + color paired (CLAUDE.md rules 5 and 6).

summary: Re-tested with chrome-devtools-mcp in Chrome 148 on the local server. Exercised sample bytes, HTTP charset select, meta charset select, editable hex, Run live iframe probe, Reset selected sample, Missing charset risk, Explicit ISO-2022-JP fix, and Header/meta conflict. Observed undeclared ISO-2022-JP bytes resolving as UTF-8 in the live iframe, explicit ISO-2022-JP resolving as ISO-2022-JP, HTTP charset winning over conflicting meta, invalid hex clearing stale outputs, and escaped script-shaped text remaining non-executable. Console was clean, network requests returned 200/204, and the feature conformance route passed 6/6.
